name: element_move
description: Reference-parity: ElementMove (xfrm.elementMove preset) — a translate weighted by an ELEMENT falloff (a sphere anchored at the picked element). Captured from a real interactive pick+drag in the reference engine: a 4x4x4 cube, the -X+Y-Z corner picked, element-falloff Range 0.5, linear shape, dragged in the screen plane. Recovered: center=(-0.5,0.5,-0.5), radius=0.5, full translate=(-1.515,1.205,0). 7 verts fall inside the sphere (corner w=1, ring@0.25 w=0.5, ring@0.354 w=0.293 = linear 1-d/0.5). Cases cover a VERTEX pick (sphere=point) an EDGE pick (segment), a POLYGON pick (face), and an EDGE-LOOPS pick (connect=edgeLoops: a picked edge expands to the whole loop ring, attenuating by distance to the ring polyline) (falloff attenuates by distance to the edge SEGMENT, anchorRing = the 2 endpoints). vibe3d replays the preset (falloff type=element + dist + ACEN.userPlaced center + TX/TY/TZ) and reproduces every vertex bit-for-bit. Built on an empty reset so the welded seg4 cube is the only geometry.
source: Frozen reference capture (xfrm.elementMove, manual interactive pick+drag via tools/local/modo_diff manual VNC; element falloff cannot be driven by synthetic input — see capture notes).

input.argstring: prim.cube cenX:0 cenY:0 cenZ:0 sizeX:1 sizeY:1 sizeZ:1 segmentsX:4 segmentsY:4 segmentsZ:4 radius:0
